_meta.top20_by_squad_value_rule: None of Iran/Iraq/Jordan/Uzbekistan/Qatar are in the top 20 by squad value (rankings: Iran #43, Uzbekistan #36, Iraq #45, Jordan #47, Qatar #46) — therefore NO tier_1_keeper entries proposed for any of these five teams.
_meta.notes: Asian Confederation teams; conservative tiering per Wave A3 instructions. Replacement names left as 'Unknown' unless a clear second-choice exists in confirmed national-team rotation.

Iran.name: Mehdi Taremi
Iran.reason: Inter Milan striker; Iran's primary attacking outlet; long-running top scorer alongside Azmoun

Iran.name: Sardar Azmoun
Iran.reason: Shabab Al-Ahli (ex-Bayer Leverkusen / Roma); co-talisman striker; Iran's joint all-time top scorer

Iraq.name: Ali Al-Hamadi
Iraq.reason: Ipswich Town (EFL Championship); Iraq's primary striker; only Iraq player at a top-flight European club at time of writing

Jordan.name: Mousa Al-Tamari
Jordan.reason: Rennes (Ligue 1); Jordan's chief creator and headline international; star of 2024 Asian Cup runner-up run

Uzbekistan.name: Eldor Shomurodov
Uzbekistan.reason: Roma / Istanbul Başakşehir (Serie A loan); Uzbekistan's primary striker and captain

Uzbekistan.name: Abdukodir Khusanov
Uzbekistan.reason: Manchester City (signed Jan 2025); primary centre-back; first Uzbek at a top-5-league title contender

Qatar.name: Akram Afif
Qatar.reason: Al-Sadd (QSL); 2024 AFC Asian Cup Player of the Tournament and top scorer; Qatar's chief creator and talisman
